samples.raw_output: {"cefr_level": "B2", "level_basis": {"textinspector_level_raw": null, "flesch_kincaid": null, "rule_notes": "Holistic assessment based on task fulfillment, register, and grammatical control."}, "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes", "communicative_task_notes": ["Response addresses the prompt, provides advice, and meets the length requirement."], "grammar_score": 0.85, "corrections": ["i had a simular problem -> I had a similar problem (Grammar; A2; Low)", "Here is the tips -> Here are the tips (Grammar; A2; Low)", "You can find this apps -> You can find these apps (Grammar; A2; Low)", "leave from useless groups -> leave useless groups (Word order; A2; Low)", "don't watch YouTube a lot! -> don't watch too much YouTube! (Register/Discourse markers; B2; Medium)"], "errors_by_category": {"Lexis": [], "Grammar": [{"excerpt": "i had a simular problem", "correction": "I had a similar problem", "comment": "Capitalization and spelling error.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}, {"excerpt": "Here is the tips", "correction": "Here are the tips", "comment": "Subject-verb agreement error.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}, {"excerpt": "You can find this apps", "correction": "You can find these apps", "comment": "Incorrect determiner.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}, {"excerpt": "It will bring you enought energy", "correction": "It will bring you enough energy", "comment": "Spelling error.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}], "Punctuation": [], "Word order": [{"excerpt": "leave from useless groups", "correction": "leave useless groups", "comment": "Incorrect preposition.", "impact": "Low", "cefr_threshold": "A2"}], "Sentence structure": [], "Text organization": [], "Discourse markers": [{"excerpt": "For more information, you can write me at personal messages", "correction": "Feel free to message me privately for more information.", "comment": "More formal and natural phrasing.", "impact": "Medium", "cefr_threshold": "B2"}], "Rhetorical features": [], "Register": [], "Incomprehensibility": []}, "strengths": [{"item": "Use special apps and calendars", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Vocabulary", "note": "Practical and relevant advice."}, {"item": "Manage your time!", "cefr_level": "B2", "category": "Discourse marker", "note": "Clear and direct advice."}], "feedback": "Level B2: The response effectively addresses the prompt, offering practical advice in a conversational, yet generally appropriate, register. {"cefr_level": "B1", "level_basis": {"textinspector_level_raw": null, "flesch_kincaid": null, "rule_notes": "Holistic assessment; frequent grammatical errors and limited vocabulary restrict level despite attempts at advice-giving."}, "communicative_task_achievement": "Yes", "communicative_task_notes": ["Response addresses the prompt, provides advice, and reaches the required length."], "grammar_score": 0.45, "corrections": ["A lot of students have the same problem. Somebody can solve it, somebody - not. -> Many students face this problem. Some can overcome it, others can’t. (Grammar/Lexis; B2; Medium)", "It is easily. -> The main goal is... (Grammar/Lexis; B1; Medium)", "Students want to become a graduate: pass all exam and finish the university. -> Students aim to graduate by passing all exams and completing their studies. (Grammar/Sentence Structure; B2; Medium)", "Now I want to some tips for became diffiecult study easilier. -> Now I’ll share some tips to make studying easier. (Grammar/Lexis; B2; Medium)", "Try to rewrite solution from other students. I agree it is bad, but if you try to understand, what your friend write and understand the main idea of solution, you can solve the same problem in future. -> Try reviewing solutions from other students. While it’s not ideal, understanding your friend’s approach can help you solve similar problems in the future. (Grammar/Lexis/Sentence Structure; B2; High)", "Do sport. It is not obviously tip, but it really work. -> Exercise. It might seem unusual, but it’s very effective. (Lexis/Grammar; B1; Medium)", "When you play, for exmaple, basketball or football your brain relax and do not do anything, so after training you can solve spend less time for doing homework than before traning. -> When you play sports like basketball or football, your brain relaxes, allowing you to spend less time on homework after training. (Grammar/Sentence Structure/Lexis; B2; High)", "More time of sleeping, more energy at work. It is obviously. -> Getting more sleep boosts your energy levels. It’s clear. (Grammar/Lexis; B1; Medium)", "If you sleep aproximately three hours, of course you do not have energy for working. -> If you only sleep for three hours, you’ll lack the energy to work effectively. (Grammar/Lexis; B2; Medium)", "Do homework after getting. It is obviously too. -> Do your homework in advance. It’s a good idea. (Grammar/Lexis; B1; Medium)", "Well, It was four tips for making you eductation esalier -> Well, these are four tips to make your education easier. (Grammar/Lexis; B1; Medium)"], "errors_by_category": {"Lexis": [], "Grammar": [{"excerpt": "A lot of students have the same problem. Somebody can solve it, somebody - not.", "correction": "Many students face this problem. The advice is generally sound, but the delivery needs substantial improvement. Strengths include the attempt to provide practical tips and the structured format of numbered points. Focus on improving sentence structure and article usage.", "next_steps": ["Practice using more precise vocabulary and avoiding direct translations from L1.", "Review basic grammar rules, especially verb tenses and subject-verb agreement.", "Focus on sentence structure and clarity to improve overall comprehensibility."]}
